mission.title: etcd: cannot find module providing package github.com/coreos/go-systemd/journal
mission.description: cannot find module providing package github.com/coreos/go-systemd/journal. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify etcd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your etcd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd -n etcd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review etcd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant etcd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd
kubectl get configmap -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=etcd
```
when i use go get -v go.etcd.io/etcd/clientv3, it shows cannot find module providing package github.com/coreos/go-systemd/journal

who know how to fix it?

steps.title: Apply the fix for cannot find module providing package…
steps.description:
[go-systemd](https://github.com/coreos/go-systemd) had completed of switching to Go modules between `v21` and `v22.0.0`. 
This pr fix mod error due to semver not being applied correctly. 
Ref. [coreos/go-systemd#321](https://github.com/coreos/go-systemd/issues/321)
```yaml
$ mkdir -p $GOPATH/src/github.com/coreos/go-systemd/
$ git clone git@github.com:coreos/go-systemd.git $GOPATH/src/github.com/coreos/go-systemd/
$ cd $myproject
$ go mod edit -replace github.com/coreos/go-systemd/=/Users/dselans/Code/go/src/github.com/coreos/go-systemd
$ go get go.etcd.io/etcd/clientv3
```

steps.title: Confirm cannot find module providing package… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd -n etcd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n etcd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
[go-systemd](https://github.com/coreos/go-systemd) had completed of switching to Go modules between `v21` and `v22.0.0`. 
This pr fix mod error due to semver not being applied correctly. 
Ref. [coreos/go-systemd#321](https://github.com/coreos/go-systemd/issues/321)
codeSnippets:
$ mkdir -p $GOPATH/src/github.com/coreos/go-systemd/
$ git clone git@github.com:coreos/go-systemd.git $GOPATH/src/github.com/coreos/go-systemd/
$ cd $myproject
$ go mod edit -replace github.com/coreos/go-systemd/=/Users/dselans/Code/go/src/github.com/coreos/go-systemd
$ go get go.etcd.io/etcd/clientv3

go get -v go.etcd.io/etcd/clientv3 
go: finding github.com/coreos/go-systemd/journal latest
go: finding github.com/coreos/go-systemd latest
../../pkg/mod/github.com/coreos/etcd@v3.3.18+incompatible/pkg/logutil/zap_journal.go:29:2: no matching versions for query "latest"

require google.golang.org/grpc v1.26.0
replace github.com/coreos/go-systemd => github.com/coreos/go-systemd/v22 v22.0.0

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with etcd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
